docs.title: the-dots
docs.text: Personal macOS development environment managed with GNU Stow and a gum-powered TUI wizard.

docs.title: Why it exists
docs.text: Keep one machine's shell config, Git identity, Homebrew apps, and macOS settings reproducible and easy to re-apply after a fresh install — without needing to remember the correct order or command for each piece.

docs.title: Status
docs.text: Stable for daily use. Covers: zsh, git, Homebrew bundles, Finder, Dock, and an interactive wizard that wraps all Make targets.

docs.title: How to start
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/thepanadev/the-dots.git ~/the-dots
cd ~/the-dots
make the-dots   # interactive wizard — installs gum if needed

The wizard reads targets directly from the Makefile and presents a menu. Every option is also runnable standalone via make <target>.

docs.title: Where to go next
docs.text:
• Reference — full Make target listing and script inventory
graph LR
    W[make the-dots] --> A[install-apps]
    W --> D[dotfiles-stow]
    W --> G[git-setup]
    W --> Z[zsh-setup]
    W --> M[finder-setup / dock-setup]

docs.title: Reference
docs.text:
This section combines short curated guidance with generated reference pages built from the Makefile and scripts.

Run ./scripts/docs/generate-all.sh before building the docs locally.

docs.title: Make Targets
docs.text:
make the-dots is the safe default — it presents a menu of all targets so you never have to remember individual commands. Use narrower targets directly when you know exactly which slice you want.

title: Make Targets provenance: derived status: stable source: scripts/docs/generate-makefile-docs.sh

Generated page

Do not edit by hand.

docs.title: Make Targets
docs.text: Target Description install-apps Install Homebrew if needed, then choose app bundles to install dotfiles-stow Choose dotfile packages to link into $HOME dotfiles-unstow Choose dotfile packages to unlink from $HOME git-setup Configure Git user.name and user.email zsh-setup Configure zsh preferences finder-setup Interactive Finder settings editor dock-setup Interactive Dock settings editor the-dots Interactive menu to run any target

docs.title: Bash Scripts
docs.text:
Scripts in scripts/ are invoked by Make targets; most are not meant to be called directly. wizard.sh and scripts/the-dots.sh are the two TUI entrypoints.

title: Bash Scripts provenance: derived status: stable source: scripts/docs/generate-bash-docs.sh

docs.title: scripts/dotfiles-stow.sh
docs.text: Choose dotfile packages to link into $HOME with GNU Stow.

docs.title: scripts/dotfiles-unstow.sh
docs.text: Choose dotfile packages to unlink from $HOME with GNU Stow.

docs.title: scripts/git-setup.sh
docs.text: Configure local Git identity for this machine.

docs.title: scripts/macos-section.sh
docs.text:
Run a macOS settings section interactively via the gum-based menu.

Usage: scripts/macos-section.sh is "finder" or "dock" (anything matching macos/.sh). ## `scripts/stow-rollback.sh` Unlink one or more stowed packages from $HOME. Usage: scripts/stow-rollback.sh [...] ## `scripts/stow.sh` Restow one or more packages from this repo into $HOME. Usage: scripts/stow.sh [...] ## `scripts/the-dots.sh` Launch the interactive dotfiles wizard, installing gum first if needed. ## `scripts/zsh-setup.sh` Configure zsh preferences that are not managed by Stow. ## `wizard.sh` Interactive TUI for the dotfiles Makefile. Parses targets, descriptions, and recipes directly from Makefile — always in sync.
